commit.message:
Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Gradle SIGSEGV crash in Docker builds on ARM (Apple Silicon)

Switch build stage from eclipse-temurin:21-jdk-alpine to eclipse-temurin:21-jdk
to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify connection path in architecture diagram

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Fix exploration: uniform policy init + stronger entropy bonus

Policy head was initialized with kaiming_uniform, biasing the
initial policy toward certain digits and trapping it in local
optima. Now initialized with small normal weights + zero bias
for near-uniform starting policy. Entropy coeff bumped to 0.1.

feat: write per-vehicle crossing events to vehicle_crossings

Analytics data collection had stopped because the vehicle_crossings
writer was removed (AnalyticsZoneProcessor was a stub). Restored by:

- counter.py: accumulate crossing_events list in process() for each
  confirmed vehicle (line zone immediate + pending flush + polygon zone).
  Each event captures track_id, vehicle_class, confidence, direction,
  scene_lighting/weather, zone_source='game'. Returned in snapshot dict.
  Added write_vehicle_crossings() async batch-insert function.

- main.py: after set_live_snapshot(), fire-and-forget
  write_vehicle_crossings() task for each frame that has crossings.
  Also exclude crossing_events from the count_snapshots write.

- traffic_daily_service.py: include zone_source 'game' alongside
  'entry' in the nightly aggregation query.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add scroll mode, prompt detection, and simplify alt screen buffer

Scroll mode: when user scrolls up, view freezes — new output no longer
snaps back to bottom. scrollOffset compensated for new scrollback lines,
trimming suppressed while frozen. Scrollbar and "Current" button overlay
for returning to live view.

Prompt detection: background goroutine scans each session screen for
shell/Claude prompt patterns. Status indicators shown in control center
tabs (▸ for shell, ● for Claude).

Alt screen buffer: simplified from save/restore to simple blank-screen
toggle (altScreen bool flag). CLAUDE.md updated to match.

commit.message:
Add breach cost estimator, AI risk narrative, maturity model, quick wins, insurance readiness

Cyber assessment dashboard now includes 5 new differentiating features:

1. Breach Cost Estimator — calculates estimated breach cost ($M), 12-month
   breach probability, and detection/containment timelines using IBM/Ponemon
   data adjusted for client industry, company size, and actual security score

2. AI Risk Narrative — auto-generates plain-English executive summary
   explaining what the scores mean, personalized to the client with specific
   dollar amounts, percentile rankings, and actionable recommendations

3. Security Maturity Model — visual 5-level maturity scale (Initial through
   Optimizing) with current level highlighted and description

4. Top 5 Quick Wins — automatically identifies highest-impact, lowest-effort
   actions ranked by ROI. Each shows estimated point improvement, effort
   level, and specific implementation guidance

5. Cyber Insurance Readiness — 12-point carrier pre-qualification checklist
   showing pass/partial/fail for each requirement insurers check, with
   overall approval likelihood verdict

Also added Client Demo link to dashboard sidebar.

Replace Anthropic API with local garden layout algorithm

Garden layouts are now generated instantly using a deterministic local
algorithm — no API calls, no timeouts, no 529 errors. The generator
groups vegetables by plant family, uses weight-based proportional cell
allocation to fill the garden, packs herbs/flowers into SubgridCells,
and produces companion notes, zone tips, care calendar, and yield
estimates from local data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Split MoGe into v1 (relative) and v2 (metric + normals) predictors

MoGeV1Predictor was incorrectly loading the v2 model and checkpoint.
This fixes it to use the actual v1 model (moge.model.v1, Ruicheng/moge-vitl)
and adds proper v2 support as separate predictors:

- MoGeV2MetricPredictor in models/metric_depth/ for metric-scale depth
- MoGeV2NormalPredictor in models/surface_normal/ for surface normals
- MoGeV2MonoPrior composite in models/monoprior/ that produces both
  metric depth and normals from a single v2 forward pass

Also moves monoprior_models.py into models/monoprior/ to be consistent
with the rest of the model directory structure, adds metric-depth and
monoprior inference APIs with Rerun visualization, pixi tasks for both,
and fixes log_normal_pred to accept real intrinsics instead of always
fabricating a fake pinhole that would overwrite correct K_33.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: improve agent color distribution for similar names (#681)

Add XOR bit-mixing step inside the DJB2 hash loop in getAgentColor()
to spread hash values across the full 32-bit range. This prevents
sequential agent names (e.g., planner-660, planner-670) from producing
clustered color indices.

Fixes #674

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Overhaul side-facing walk animation with authentic GB sprites, expand map

- Replace cursed left/right walk sprites with real Pokemon Red/Blue data
  from pokered decompilation (Frame 2 standing, Frame 5 walking)
- Generate right-facing sprites at runtime by flipping left-facing ones
- Add 2-frame walk cycle: stride for 6 frames, standing for last 2
- Add generate_side_sprites.py tool for reproducible sprite generation
- Fix duplicate nidoran keys in pokemon data (nidoranF/nidoranM)

commit.message:
Fix LLM reranker: strict RESULT-line parsing, reject irrelevant sources

The reranker was keeping garbage results (Dietary Reference Intakes for
food allergy questions, AI publications for medical questions) because:
1. Prompt wasn't aggressive enough about rejecting
2. Parser extracted numbers from reasoning text, not just the RESULT line

Now: structured TOPIC/RESULT format forces explicit relevance judgment.
Parser only reads the RESULT line. Missing RESULT = no results (safe).

Before: IgG food sensitivity got 10 "sources" (all Dietary Reference Intakes)
After: correctly returns 0, becomes NASEM gap with CDC/AAAI alternatives

fix(stitcher): add -reset_timestamps 1 to trim — fixes audio-only output

When trimming with -c copy, output timestamps retain the original file
offset (e.g. segment starting at 0.54s has DTS 0.54s). The concat
demuxer sees a timestamp gap between segments and drops the video stream,
producing an audio-only file. -reset_timestamps 1 resets each trimmed
segment to start at DTS 0, so the concat muxer can join them cleanly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix OTP: switch to Termii Token API, no registered sender needed

The regular Termii SMS API requires sender IDs (N-Alert, CartY) to be
registered on the account, which they aren't. Switched to Termii's
dedicated Token OTP API (/api/sms/otp/send + /api/sms/otp/verify):

- send_sms_otp: calls /api/sms/otp/send (Termii generates + sends the PIN)
  returns (True, pin_id) on success - no registered sender needed
- store_otp: stores 'TERMII:{pin_id}' as the code in otp_codes table
- verify_otp_code: detects TERMII: prefix, calls /api/sms/otp/verify
  with the stored pin_id and the user-entered code
- register + send_otp_endpoint callers updated to new signature
  (send first to get pin_id, then store it)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  pointer_address and base_data_address per meter
- internal/config/types.go: MedidorConfig struct + Medidores field on
  StationConfig (backward-compatible; single-meter stations unchanged)
- internal/modbus/decoders.go: DateRaw + TimeRaw fields on HourRecord
  (populated from bytes 0-1 and 2-3 of each ROC circular-buffer record)
- internal/api/handlers/sync.go: syncTask struct + expandTasks() expand
  multi-medidor stations into N independent sync tasks; task key format
  "STATION / M1" used for progress events and result keys
- frontend/src/stores/sync.js: stationsExpected populated lazily from
  incoming progress events (task keys, not station names); retryStation
  parses "STATION / M1" key to look up correct medidor db_address
- frontend/src/components/sync/MultiSignalChart.vue: parseRocDate()
  decodes ROC date/time registers; X-axis ticks show actual device
  date (DD/MM) + time (HH:mm) when available; tooltip shows real
  date/time string instead of synthetic Ptr-based label
